sources.exact_document_title: Practice Guidelines for Preoperative Fasting and the Use of Pharmacologic Agents to Reduce the Risk of Pulmonary Aspiration
sources.population: Healthy patients of all ages undergoing elective procedures requiring anesthesia or sedation, with stated modifying conditions.
sources.clinical_setting: Preprocedure fasting assessment for elective anesthesia or sedation.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for preoperative record review, interview, fasting compliance, and aspiration-risk modifiers. Fasting durations, medicine instructions, and management are not mapped.

exact_sections.heading: Preoperative assessment of fasting status and compliance
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports medical-record review, patient interview, verification of fasting status, and documentation of compliance.

exact_sections.heading: Population and aspiration-risk modifiers
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports noting pregnancy, obesity, diabetes, reflux, obstruction, emergency, and difficult-airway context as assessment qualifiers only.

archive.title: Practice Guidelines for Preoperative Fasting and the Use of Pharmacologic Agents to Reduce the Risk of Pulmonary Aspiration
archive.population: Healthy patients of all ages undergoing elective procedures requiring anesthesia or sedation, with stated modifying conditions.
archive.setting: Preprocedure fasting assessment for elective anesthesia or sedation.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Preoperative assessment of fasting status and compliance
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports medical-record review, patient interview, verification of fasting status, and documentation of compliance.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Population and aspiration-risk modifiers
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports noting pregnancy, obesity, diabetes, reflux, obstruction, emergency, and difficult-airway context as assessment qualifiers only.

sources.exact_document_title: Guidelines for the Provision of Anaesthesia Services: Chapter 2 — Guidelines for the Provision of Anaesthesia Services for the Perioperative Care of Elective and Urgent Care Patients 2025
sources.population: Elective and urgent surgical patients requiring perioperative anesthesia assessment and care.
sources.clinical_setting: Preoperative assessment, perioperative planning, high-risk review, and referral pathways.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for universal preoperative assessment, previous anesthesia records and alerts, risk domains, high-risk documentation, repeat assessment, medicines, lifestyle, pain, function, and local policies. UAE pathways and thresholds require local adaptation.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 2.1–2.2 — assessment and specialist review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports assessment before anesthesia or sedation and clinician review of concerns, results, risk, and shared decisions.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 3.6 and 4.1–4.4 — previous records and risk domains
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports previous anesthesia records and alerts plus age, comorbidity, medicines, allergy, surgery, pain, cognition, frailty, nutrition, lifestyle, psychological, functional, and chronic-pain risk assessment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 4.2 and 4.6 — high-risk documentation and local policies
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documented high-risk review and local policies for tests, blood, fasting, medicines, escalation, referral, and pregnancy without generating an action.
